channel.title: Go2 Thailand
channel.description: Your ultimate guide to traveling Thailand - tips, itineraries, food guides, and more.

item.title: Anantara Siam Bangkok Nears Completion of Its USD 50 Million Transformation
item.description: Anantara Siam Bangkok is in the middle of a THB 1.6 billion renovation that will add private-pool garden suites, a redesigned lobby and a refreshed Spice Market restaurant. Here is what is changing.

item.title: Andaz One Bangkok: Inside the Luxury Hotel Next to Lumphini Park (2026)
item.description: The Andaz One Bangkok opened in late 2025 as part of Thailand's biggest mixed-use development. Here is what to expect from the rooms, restaurants, rooftop pool and surrounding neighbourhood.

item.title: Ayutthaya Day Trip by Train from Bangkok: A Budget Temple-Hopping Guide for 2026
item.description: Take the train from Bangkok to Ayutthaya and explore ancient temple ruins for under 500 baht. Here is everything you need to know about planning the perfect Ayutthaya day trip in 2026.

item.title: Bangkok to Chiang Mai Sleeper Train: The Complete 2026 Guide
item.description: How to book the overnight sleeper train from Bangkok to Chiang Mai in 2026, including ticket prices, carriage types, schedule and practical tips.

item.title: Bangkok's Craft Beer Scene in 2026: Festivals, Taprooms and Where to Drink
item.description: Bangkok's craft beer scene has grown fast. From the People Festival with 400-plus brands to neighbourhood taprooms pouring Thai-brewed beers, here is where to drink in 2026.

item.title: Bangkok Public Transport in 2026: BTS, MRT, and Monorails Explained for Tourists
item.description: Bangkok's rail network keeps expanding. Here is the full 2026 guide to the BTS Skytrain, MRT subway, Yellow and Pink monorails, and how to use them as a tourist.

item.title: Bangkok's Specialty Coffee Scene in 2026: Where to Find the Best Cafés and Roasters
item.description: Bangkok has quietly become one of Asia's top specialty coffee cities. From micro-roasters in Ari to award-winning baristas in Thonglor, here is where to drink great coffee in the Thai capital in 2026.

item.title: Bangkok Spectacular 2026: AWC Launches Cultural Tourism Push Along the Chao Phraya
item.description: Asset World Corporation's Bangkok Spectacular 2026 initiative brings new cultural attractions to the Chao Phraya riverfront, headlined by the opening of the THAINOSAUR Museum.

item.title: Bangkok Top Tables 2026: The City's Best Restaurants According to BK Magazine
item.description: BK Magazine has revealed its Top Tables 2026 list of Bangkok's best restaurants. From Cote by Mauro Colagreco at No. 1 to Baan Tepa's Best Thai honour, here is what foodies need to know.

item.title: Best Cooking Classes in Bangkok with Market Tours in 2026
item.description: A hands-on guide to the best cooking classes in Bangkok for 2026, from Silom market tours to canal-side home kitchens, with prices, what you will cook, and how to book.

item.title: Best Kayaking and Paddleboarding Spots in Thailand for 2026
item.description: From Krabi's limestone cliffs to Ang Thong's emerald lagoons, here are the best places to kayak and paddleboard in Thailand in 2026, with prices, seasons, and practical tips.

item.title: Best Party Hostels in Thailand 2026: Bangkok, the Islands and Chiang Mai
item.description: A guide to the best party hostels across Thailand in 2026, from Khao San Road pub crawls to Full Moon pre-parties on Koh Phangan, with real prices and tips.

item.title: Best SIM Card and eSIM for Thailand in 2026: A Tourist's Complete Guide
item.description: Everything you need to know about buying a SIM card or eSIM for Thailand in 2026 — providers, prices, airport pickup, 5G coverage, and which option is best for tourists.

item.title: BTS World Tour Bangkok December 2026: Dates, Venue and Ticket Guide
item.description: BTS is coming to Bangkok on December 3, 5 and 6, 2026 as part of their Arirang World Tour. Here is everything we know about tickets, the venue and travel tips.

item.title: Camping in Thailand's National Parks: A Practical Guide for 2026
item.description: Everything you need to know about camping in Thailand's national parks in 2026 — from fees and gear rental to the best parks for pitching a tent.

item.title: Cape Fahn Hotel: A Private Island Escape Off Koh Samui
item.description: Cape Fahn Hotel sits on its own private island just off Koh Samui's northeast coast — 22 pool villas, a Michelin-starred chef, and the option to book the entire island for yourself.

item.title: Chanthaburi: Thailand's Hidden Gem Capital on the Eastern Coast
item.description: Chanthaburi is one of eastern Thailand's best-kept secrets. From one of the world's largest gem markets to a charming riverside old town, here is why this province deserves a spot on your 2026 itinerary.

item.title: Chiang Dao: Stargazing and Eco-Lodges in Thailand's Mountain Sanctuary
item.description: Just 90 minutes north of Chiang Mai, Chiang Dao offers dark-sky stargazing, eco-conscious lodges, and mountain scenery that rivals anywhere in Southeast Asia. Here is your 2026 guide.

item.title: Chiang Mai's Hidden Lanna Temple Walking Trail: 5 Temples Tourists Miss in 2026
item.description: Skip the crowds at Doi Suthep and walk Chiang Mai's lesser-known Lanna temple trail instead. Five hidden temples with ancient tunnels, forest paths and zero tour buses.

item.title: Chiang Mai Street Art: A Self-Guided Walking Tour Through the Old City, Wualai and Nimman
item.description: Explore Chiang Mai's vibrant street art scene on foot. This self-guided walking tour covers the Old City, Wualai and Nimmanhaemin with tips on the best murals and local artists.

item.title: Dib Bangkok: Thailand's First International Contemporary Art Museum Opens in Khlong Toei
item.description: Dib Bangkok is Thailand's first international contemporary art museum, housed in a repurposed 1980s warehouse in Khlong Toei. Opening exhibition runs until August 2026.

item.title: Durian Season in Thailand 2026: Where to Eat the King of Fruits
item.description: Thailand's durian season runs from May to August, peaking in June. Here is where to find the best durian in Bangkok, Rayong and Chanthaburi in 2026.

item.title: Earth Hour 2026 in Bangkok: Which Landmarks Go Dark on March 28
item.description: Bangkok joins the 20th anniversary of Earth Hour on March 28, 2026. Find out which landmarks switch off their lights and how travellers can take part.

item.title: El Nino 2026: How Thailand's Weather Is Changing and What Tourists Should Know
item.description: El Nino is expected to develop from May 2026, delaying Thailand's monsoon and pushing temperatures above 42 degrees Celsius. Here is what it means for your trip and how to stay safe.

item.title: Erawan Falls Day Trip Guide: Seven Tiers of Turquoise Pools in Kanchanaburi
item.description: Everything you need to know before visiting Erawan Falls in 2026: entrance fees, park rules, how to reach all seven tiers, and tips for avoiding the crowds.

item.title: Fairmont Bangkok Sukhumvit: Thailand's First Fairmont Hotel Opens June 2026
Foreign Investors Pour 12 Billion Baht Into Thai Hotels as Confidence Surges
item.description: Foreign hotel investment in Thailand is on track to exceed 12 billion baht in 2026, with buyers targeting Bangkok, Phuket and Koh Samui properties.

item.title: HYROX Bangkok 2026: The World's Biggest Fitness Race Lands at BITEC
item.description: HYROX Bangkok 2026 drew over 17,500 athletes to BITEC from March 20-22. Here is everything you need to know about Southeast Asia's largest fitness racing event.

item.title: Kahavadi Chiang Rai: Hilton's Curio Collection River Retreat Opens July 2026
KAIA Koh Phangan: Thailand's First Ultra-Luxury Eco Tented Resort Opens in 2026
item.description: KAIA Koh Phangan is a new ultra-luxury tented resort opening in 2026 on the Gulf of Thailand, combining barefoot luxury with serious eco credentials. Here is what we know.

item.title: Khao Yai Food & Wine Festival 2026: A Vineyard Day Trip from Bangkok
item.description: The Khao Yai Food & Wine Festival 2026 puts Thailand's vineyard country in the spotlight. Learn about the wineries, Korat GI products and how to plan a day trip from Bangkok.

item.title: Khao Yai National Park: The Ultimate Day Trip From Bangkok (2026 Guide)
item.description: Everything you need to know about visiting Khao Yai National Park in 2026, from entrance fees and transport to the best trails, waterfalls and wildlife spotting tips.

item.title: Koh Kood: Thailand's Quietest Island Escape in 2026
item.description: Koh Kood is Thailand's fourth-largest island, yet it remains one of the least visited. Here is everything you need to know about getting there, where to stay, and what to expect in 2026.

item.title: Koh Larn Day Trip from Pattaya: Beaches, Ferries and Practical Tips for 2026
item.description: Koh Larn sits just 7 km off Pattaya with clear water, white sand and cheap ferries. Here is everything you need to plan a day trip in 2026, from ferry times to the best beaches.

item.title: Koh Lipe 2026: Complete Guide to the Maldives of Thailand
item.description: Koh Lipe is a tiny island in the Tarutao National Marine Park often called the Maldives of Thailand. Here is everything you need to know for a 2026 visit, from beaches and diving to ferries and budget tips.

item.title: Koh Samui Airport Is Becoming Thailand's Second International Hub in 2026
item.description: Bangkok Airways is transforming Koh Samui Airport into Thailand's second international flight hub with new routes to Abu Dhabi, Dubai, Shanghai and more. Here is what travellers need to know.

item.title: K-pop Concerts in Bangkok 2026: SEVENTEEN, TREASURE, BTS and More
item.description: Bangkok is a major stop on every K-pop world tour in 2026. Here is the full schedule of confirmed concerts including SEVENTEEN, TREASURE and the long-awaited BTS return in December.

item.title: Kraftwerk's First-Ever Thailand Concert: Multimedia Tour Hits Bangkok in May 2026
item.description: Kraftwerk bring their legendary Multimedia Tour to Bangkok on May 10, 2026 — marking the electronic pioneers' first-ever performance in Thailand. Here is everything you need to know about tickets, venue, and the show.

item.title: Mahanakhon I-Tilt: Bangkok's 296-Metre Glass Platform That Tilts You Over the Edge
item.description: The Mahanakhon I-Tilt is the world's only outdoor tilting glass platform, suspending visitors 296 metres above Bangkok at a 65-degree angle. Here is everything you need to know before you go.

item.title: Muay Thai Training Camps in Thailand: A Beginner's Guide for 2026
item.description: From Phuket to Bangkok and Chiang Mai, Thailand's Muay Thai camps welcome complete beginners. Here is what to expect, what it costs and the best camps to train at in 2026.

item.title: Nasatta Light Festival 2026: A Fairy-Tale Night Out in Ratchaburi
item.description: The Nasatta Light Festival 2026 transforms 30 rai of woodland in Ratchaburi into a glowing fairy-tale landscape. Open nightly until May 3 — here is everything visitors need to know.

item.title: New Airline Routes to Thailand in 2026: flydubai, Norse Atlantic and Scoot Lead the Charge
item.description: Multiple international carriers are launching new routes to Thailand in 2026, including flydubai, Norse Atlantic Airways and Scoot with direct flights to Chiang Rai.

item.title: 3 New Luxury Resorts Opening in Thailand in 2026
item.description: Three major resort brands are opening new properties in Thailand in 2026 — JW Marriott in Phuket, a Hilton Tapestry Collection on Koh Samui, and Grand Mercure in Krabi.

item.title: Old Town Phuket Walking Guide: Sino-Portuguese Streets, Murals and Hidden Cafés
item.description: Skip the beach for a morning and explore Old Town Phuket on foot. This walking guide covers the best Sino-Portuguese streets, murals, cafés and markets in Phuket's colourful historic quarter.

item.title: Pattaya Music Festival 2026: Free Beach Concerts Every Weekend in March
item.description: The Pattaya Music Festival 2026 brings four weekends of free seaside concerts to Pattaya Beach, Jomtien, Koh Larn and Naklua. Here is the full schedule and what to expect.

item.title: Pattaya Pride and Circuit Festival Asia 2026: Thailand's Biggest LGBTQ+ Events This June
item.description: Pattaya hosts two major LGBTQ+ events in June 2026: Circuit Festival Asia (June 19-21) and Pattaya International Pride Festival (June 26-28). Here is everything travellers need to know.

item.title: The Pattaya Paradox: Fewer Tourists, More Revenue in Thailand's Beach City
item.description: Despite strong national tourism numbers, Pattaya's streets feel quieter than expected. Yet the city is earning more than before, driven by high-spending Russian and European visitors. Here is what is happening.

item.title: Pattaya Walking Street Faces Calls for Reinvention as Visitor Preferences Shift
item.description: Pattaya's iconic Walking Street is struggling with quieter nights and harsh criticism from tourists, prompting city leaders to explore a broader reinvention of the famous nightlife strip.

item.title: Phra Pradaeng Songkran 2026: Bangkok's Mon-Culture Water Festival on April 24-26
item.description: Phra Pradaeng holds its own Songkran festival on April 24-26, 2026 — a week after the national holiday. Discover its Mon-heritage traditions, parades and how to get there from Bangkok.

item.title: The Prince Akatoki Riverside Bangkok: Japanese Luxury Arrives on the Chao Phraya
item.description: The Prince Akatoki Riverside Bangkok is set to open in late 2026 with 255 rooms on the Chao Phraya River, bringing Japanese luxury hospitality to Thailand for the first time.

item.title: Rava Beach Club: Thailand's Longest Beach Club Opens on Phuket's BangTao Beach
item.description: Rava Beach Club by Banyan Tree Phuket stretches 150 metres along BangTao Beach, making it Thailand's longest beach club. Three infinity pools, live DJs and flame-grilled dining await.

item.title: Red Lotus Sea in Udon Thani Breaks Visitor Records Thanks to the 'Lisa Effect'
item.description: The 'Lisa Effect' has turned Udon Thani's Red Lotus Sea into one of Southeast Asia's hottest attractions, with TAT forecasting 120,000 visitors for the 2025-2026 bloom season.

item.title: S2O Songkran Music Festival 2026: Bangkok's Wildest Water and EDM Party
item.description: S2O Songkran Music Festival returns to Bangkok on April 11-13, 2026 with Zedd, Kygo, Alan Walker and more. Here is everything you need to know about tickets, lineup and what to expect.

item.title: Sak Yant Bamboo Tattoo in Thailand: What to Know Before You Go in 2026
item.description: Thinking about getting a Sak Yant bamboo tattoo in Thailand? Here is everything you need to know in 2026, from choosing a monk or ajarn to designs, costs and hygiene tips.

item.title: Singapore Airlines Eyes Nonstop Flights to Chiang Mai in 2026
SkyFlyers at Asiatique Bangkok: Asia-Pacific's Tallest Giant Swing Ride
item.description: SkyFlyers: Wings of Garudapterus at Asiatique Bangkok is Asia-Pacific's tallest giant swing ride at 135 metres. Tickets, hours, tips and combo deals for 2026.

item.title: Songkran 2026 Goes Nationwide: Thailand's Culture Ministry Rolls Out Celebrations in All 76 Provinces
item.description: Thailand's Culture Ministry is bringing Songkran 2026 to all 76 provinces under a new nationwide campaign. Here is what to expect beyond Bangkok, Chiang Mai and Pattaya.

item.title: Songkran 2026 Water Gun Rules: What Is Banned and What Can Get You Fined
item.description: Thailand is enforcing strict Songkran 2026 rules including bans on high-pressure water guns and throwing water from vehicles. Fines can reach 70,000 baht.

item.title: Space & Time Cube Bangkok: Inside Thailand's Immersive Metaverse Museum
item.description: Space & Time Cube in Bangkok is a 1,500 sqm immersive metaverse museum with 27 themed rooms, 720-degree projections, and glasses-free 3D holograms. Here is what visitors should know.

item.title: Tha Kha Floating Market: Thailand's Most Authentic Canal Market in 2026
item.description: Skip the tourist crowds and visit Tha Kha Floating Market in Samut Songkhram — one of Thailand's last truly local canal markets where vendors still paddle garden-fresh produce to buyers every weekend.

item.title: Thai Airways Launches A321neo Fleet Overhaul With 32 New Jets Rolling Out in 2026
item.description: Thai Airways is receiving 32 new Airbus A321neo aircraft throughout 2026, bringing flat-bed business class and lower emissions to regional routes across Asia.

item.title: Thai Airways Raises Fares 10-15% as Jet Fuel Costs Surge in 2026
item.description: Thai Airways has raised ticket prices by 10 to 15 percent amid soaring jet fuel costs, while Bangkok Airways and Nok Air lobby for a temporary fuel tax exemption. Here is what it means for travelers heading to Thailand.

item.title: Thai Airways Summer 2026: New Direct Flights to Amsterdam, Auckland and Perth
item.description: Thai Airways unveils its Summer 2026 schedule with new nonstop routes from Bangkok to Amsterdam, Auckland and Perth, plus expanded frequencies across Asia.

item.title: Thai Airlines Sound Alarm: Fuel Surge Could Slow 2026 Tourism Boom
Thai Airlines Push Government to Slash Jet Fuel Tax From 4.73 to 0.20 Baht per Litre
item.description: The Airlines Association of Thailand proposes cutting the domestic jet fuel excise tax from 4.73 to 0.20 baht per litre. If approved, ticket prices could drop and millions of extra seats could open up.

item.title: 13 New Bib Gourmand Restaurants in Thailand for 2026: Where to Eat on a Budget
item.description: The Michelin Guide Thailand 2026 added 13 new Bib Gourmand restaurants across Bangkok, Chiang Mai, Surat Thani and more. Here are the best new affordable eats worth a detour.

item.title: Thailand Shelves Casino Legalization After Anutin's Re-election
item.description: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul has effectively killed Thailand's casino legalization plans. Here is what happened and what it means for tourism.

item.title: TAT Bets Big on Domestic Travel to Offset Global Tourism Headwinds
item.description: The Tourism Authority of Thailand is doubling down on domestic travel, targeting 210 million trips and 1 trillion baht in revenue as global headwinds slow international arrivals.

item.title: Thailand Faces Major Drought in 2026 — What Travellers Should Know
item.description: Thailand is bracing for a significant drought in 2026 with a projected water shortage of over 4,450 million cubic metres. Here is what it means for tourists.

item.title: 41 Thai Destinations Earn 'Thailand Good Travel' Sustainability Certification
item.description: Thailand has awarded its new 'Thailand Good Travel' sustainability certification to 41 destinations and tourism operators, part of the Green Tourism Plan 2030.

item.title: Hands-Free Travel in Thailand: The 2026 Trend That Lets You Ditch Your Luggage
item.description: Hands-free travel is taking off in Thailand. Luggage delivery services let you explore Bangkok, Phuket, and Chiang Mai bag-free from the moment you land.

item.title: Thailand's Hotel Sector Hits Record Pipeline in 2026 as Luxury Brands Race to Open
item.description: Bangkok leads the Asia Pacific hotel construction pipeline with 68 projects and over 16,600 rooms. Here is what the luxury surge means for travelers visiting Thailand in 2026.

item.title: Thailand Jay Food Guide: How to Find Cheap Vegan Street Food With the Yellow Flag
item.description: Jay food is Thailand's answer to affordable vegan street food. Look for the yellow flag with red เจ script for plant-based meals from 50 THB. A practical guide for travellers in 2026.

item.title: Thailand Liveaboard Diving Guide 2026: Similan Islands, Surin & Richelieu Rock
item.description: Everything you need to know about liveaboard diving in Thailand for the 2025-2026 season, from the Similan Islands and Richelieu Rock to brand-new boats like the DiveRACE Class X.

item.title: Thailand Rolls Out Tourism Stimulus Package: Tax Breaks and Low-Season Travel Incentives for 2026
item.description: Thailand's government under PM Anutin has approved tourism stimulus measures including personal income tax deductions for domestic travel and reduced entertainment venue taxes. Here is how it could benefit visitors in 2026.

item.title: Thailand Redirects Tourism Marketing Toward Short-Haul Asian Markets
item.description: With long-haul arrivals disrupted by Middle East conflicts, Thailand is shifting its tourism marketing focus to neighboring Asian countries and short-haul travelers.

item.title: Quietcation: Thailand Embraces the Tranquil Travel Trend in 2026
Thailand Bets on Silver Tourism: Active Seniors Become Key Target Market in 2026
item.description: Thailand's tourism industry is pivoting to attract active seniors aged 50-70, one of the fastest-growing travel segments globally. Here is what the shift means for visitors.

item.title: Thailand Faces Stiff Tourism Competition as Vietnam and Neighbours Gain Ground in 2026
item.description: Vietnam's international arrivals grew over 20 percent in 2025 while Thailand's fell 7.2 percent. Here is how the competitive landscape is shifting and what it means for travelers.

item.title: Thailand Tourism Festival 2026: The '5 Must Do' Food and Culture Highlights
item.description: The 44th Thailand Tourism Festival runs March 25-29 at QSNCC Bangkok, built around the '5 Must Do in Thailand' concept: Must Taste, Must Try, Must Buy, Must Seek, and Must See.

item.title: Thailand Tourism Festival 2026: A Food Lover's Guide to Regional Flavours at QSNCC
item.description: The 44th Thailand Tourism Festival at QSNCC Bangkok features regional food from all five parts of the country. Here is what to eat and why it matters.

item.title: Thailand Tourism Festival 2026: Your Practical Guide to the 'Feel All the Feelings' Event
item.description: The 44th Thailand Tourism Festival runs March 25-29 at QSNCC Bangkok. Here is everything you need to know: zones, deals, how to get there, and what to expect.

item.title: Thailand Targets 10% Tourism Growth in 2026 After a Tough 2025
item.description: Thailand is aiming for more than 10 percent growth in foreign tourist arrivals in 2026 after a 7.23 percent decline last year. Here is what the recovery plan looks like.

item.title: Thailand Tourism Outlook 2026: Balancing Hopes, Pressure and Uncertainty
Thailand Tourism Shifts Gears in 2026: Fewer Visitors, Better Experiences
item.description: Thailand is seeing fewer international arrivals in 2026 but is treating it as an opportunity. The focus is shifting to longer stays, higher spending, and better experiences for visitors.

item.title: Thailand Cuts Visa-Free Stay From 60 to 30 Days: What Tourists Need to Know
item.description: Thailand's government is planning to cut the visa-free stay from 60 days back to 30 days. Here is what the change means for tourists and when it takes effect.

item.title: Thailand Is Quietly Ending the Visa Run Era in 2026 — What You Need to Know
item.description: Thailand immigration is cracking down on serial visa runners in 2026. Here is what changed, who is affected, and the legal alternatives for long-stay visitors.

item.title: Thailand Launches 'The New Thailand' Vision at ITB Berlin 2026
item.description: Thailand debuted its ambitious 'The New Thailand' campaign at ITB Berlin 2026, putting wellness, sustainability, and off-the-beaten-path destinations at the centre of its global tourism pitch.

item.title: TOYOTA Thailand Open 2026: World-Class Badminton Returns to Bangkok in May
item.description: The TOYOTA Thailand Open 2026 brings elite badminton back to Bangkok's Nimibutr Arena from 12 to 17 May, with a USD 500,000 prize pool and top-ranked players from around the world.

item.title: U-Tapao Pattaya Airport Is Getting New Flights and a Second Runway in 2026
item.description: U-Tapao Rayong-Pattaya airport is expanding fast in 2026 with a new runway under construction and direct flights launching to Kazakhstan, China, and Malaysia.

item.title: voco Bangkok Surawong: IHG Opens Its First voco Hotel in Thailand
item.description: IHG Hotels & Resorts has opened voco Bangkok Surawong, the first voco-branded property in Thailand. Here is what guests can expect from this heritage-meets-modern hotel on Surawong Road.

item.title: Wildlife Conservation Volunteering in Thailand 2026: Turtles, Elephants and Rescue Centres
item.description: Want to give back while visiting Thailand? Here is a guide to the best wildlife conservation volunteer programmes in 2026, from sea turtle research in Trang to ethical elephant sanctuaries in Phuket.

item.title: Best Yoga Retreats on Koh Samui and Koh Phangan in 2026
item.description: A practical guide to the best yoga and meditation retreats on Koh Samui and Koh Phangan in 2026, with prices, styles and booking tips.

item.title: AirAsia and TAT Launch 'Fly Your Feelings' Campaign with Lisa-Inspired Routes
item.description: AirAsia partners with the Tourism Authority of Thailand for the Fly Your Feelings campaign, offering 15% off flights to 8 provinces and a chance to win free flights for a year.

item.title: Thailand Launches AI-Powered Travel App with Mastercard Integration
item.description: Thailand's revamped Amazing Thailand app launched March 15, featuring AI-powered trip planning, Mastercard Priceless experiences, and real-time travel assistance.

item.title: Andamanda Phuket Hosts a Massive Songkran Pool Party on April 13-15, 2026
item.description: Andamanda Phuket turns Songkran into a three-day pool party from April 13-15, 2026 with DJs, wave pools, and waterslides. Here is what to expect and how to book.

item.title: Banana Beach on Koh Hey Named Asia's Most Beautiful Beach in 2026
item.description: Banana Beach on Koh Hey near Phuket tops Tripadvisor's 2026 Travelers' Choice Awards as Asia's most beautiful beach. Here's what to expect and how to visit.

item.title: Bangkok Art Biennale 2026: Angels and Mara Transforms the City Into an Open-Air Gallery
item.description: The Bangkok Art Biennale 2026 runs from October 2026 to February 2027 with the theme Angels and Mara. Free art installations across temples, museums and landmarks.

item.title: Bangkok Named Best City in Asia for 2026 by DestinAsian Readers
item.description: Bangkok wins Best City in Asia for the third consecutive year in DestinAsian's 2026 Readers' Choice Awards, beating Tokyo and Singapore with over 1 million votes.

item.title: Bangkok's Best Revamped Cocktail Bars to Visit in March 2026
item.description: Three of Bangkok's best bars have reinvented their drink menus in early 2026. From rooftop reinventions to hidden speakeasies, here is where to drink in Bangkok right now.

item.title: Bangkok's Best Floating Markets in 2026: Damnoen Saduak, Amphawa and Beyond
item.description: A practical guide to the best floating markets near Bangkok in 2026, including Damnoen Saduak, Amphawa, and lesser-known alternatives with eco-friendly updates.

item.title: Bangkok's New Lumpini Hawker Centre: 88 Street Food Stalls Next to the Park
item.description: Bangkok's first hawker centre opens near Lumpini Park in 2026, bringing 88 street food vendors under one roof on Ratchadamri Road. Here is what to expect.

item.title: 3 New Bangkok Rooftop Bars Worth the Elevator Ride in 2026
item.description: Three brand-new rooftop bars have opened in Bangkok in 2026 — Sato San, Sanctuary, and Iris. Here is what each one offers and whether they are worth a visit.

item.title: Bangkok Unveils Safety Plan for Khaosan Road Songkran 2026
item.description: Bangkok officials announce comprehensive safety measures for Songkran 2026 on Khaosan Road, including CCTV monitoring, crowd control, and the '5 Por' campaign.

item.title: Bangkok's Yellow and Pink Line Monorails: A Tourist Guide for 2026
item.description: Bangkok's driverless Yellow and Pink Line monorails opened in 2023-2024 and make it easy to reach neighbourhoods tourists used to skip. Here is how to use them in 2026.

item.title: Bangla Road Gets a Digital Makeover: Phuket's Nightlife Enters a New Era
item.description: Bangla Road in Patong has undergone a massive digital transformation with AR menus, LED stages, and cashless payments. Here is what the new Phuket nightlife looks like in 2026.

item.title: Banphe & Samed Island Festival 2026: Rayong's Big Seafood Party
item.description: The Banphe & Samed Island Festival 2026 runs March 11-15 in Rayong with 300+ seafood vendors, free nightly concerts, and easy access to Koh Samet.

item.title: Banthat Thong Road: Bangkok's Hottest Food Street in 2026
item.description: Banthat Thong Road near Chulalongkorn University has become Bangkok's trendiest street food strip in 2026. Here is what to eat, when to go, and how to get there.

item.title: The Barai Hua Hin: Hyatt's New Wellness Resort Opens in Late 2026
item.description: The Barai Hua Hin joins Hyatt's Unbound Collection in late 2026, bringing 90 rooms, an 18-room destination spa and a Thai healing philosophy to the royal beach town.

item.title: The Blue Jasmine: Thailand's New Luxury Train From Bangkok to Chiang Mai
item.description: The Blue Jasmine is a restored 1960s sleeper train offering a nine-day luxury journey from Bangkok to Chiang Mai, with just 37 guests per departure. Here is everything you need to know.

item.title: Bun Bang Fai: Thailand's Explosive Rocket Festival in Yasothon (May 2026)
item.description: Every May, the people of Yasothon in northeast Thailand launch giant homemade rockets into the sky to summon the rains. Here is everything you need to know about Bun Bang Fai 2026.

item.title: Cave Fantasy at MBK Center: Bangkok's New Immersive Digital Art Museum
item.description: Cave Fantasy opened in January 2026 on the 4th floor of MBK Center in Bangkok, offering nine themed galleries, glasses-free immersive visuals, and a VR flight simulator.

item.title: Central Phuket Announces $836 Million Expansion Into a Luxury Shopping Destination
item.description: Central Phuket unveils an $836 million expansion that will grow the mall by 40 percent, adding luxury flagship stores, a Mitsukoshi food hall and a 5.5-acre entertainment zone by 2028.

item.title: Chiang Mai Named World's Cheapest Digital Nomad City in 2026
item.description: A February 2026 study ranks Chiang Mai as the most affordable digital nomad destination on the planet, with rent under $400 and coworking from $85 a month.

item.title: Chiang Rai White Temple Entrance Fee Doubles to 200 Baht in 2026
item.description: The White Temple in Chiang Rai doubled its entrance fee to 200 baht for foreign visitors from January 2026. Here is what to know before visiting, plus a free alternative nearby.

item.title: Chilli Fest 2026: Bangkok's Spiciest Food Festival at Kimpton Maa-Lai
item.description: Chilli Fest 2026 returns to Kimpton Maa-Lai Bangkok on March 28 with 16 Michelin-recognized restaurants, a 2.2-million-Scoville eating contest, and free chilli tattoos.

item.title: 5 Ethical Elephant Sanctuaries in Thailand: A Responsible Visitor's Guide for 2026
item.description: Want to see elephants in Thailand without supporting exploitation? These five ethical sanctuaries in Chiang Mai, Phuket, Krabi and beyond offer observation-only or minimal-contact experiences in 2026.

item.title: Exercise Cope Tiger 2026: Massive Military Air Drill at Korat Air Base
item.description: The 32nd Exercise Cope Tiger brings Singapore, Thailand and US air forces together at Korat Air Base in March 2026 for large-scale air combat training and community outreach.

item.title: Global Wellness Summit 2026 Comes to Phuket: What Travellers Should Know
item.description: The 20th Global Wellness Summit takes place in Phuket from 10-13 November 2026. Here is what it means for Thailand's wellness tourism push and how visitors can benefit.

item.title: Harbor Island: Bangkok's Massive Rooftop Waterpark With Slides, Ziplines and a Lazy River
item.description: Harbor Island is Southeast Asia's largest rooftop waterpark — 10,000 square meters of slides, a lazy river, and a 100-meter zipline on top of a Bangkok shopping mall.

item.title: Thailand Launches 'Healing Journey' Global Wellness Tourism Campaign
item.description: Thailand's Tourism Authority launches the Healing Journey Thailand campaign globally, positioning wellness and mindfulness as the new luxury travel experience for 2026.

item.title: Khao Sok Floating Bungalows: Sleep on a Lake in the Thai Jungle
item.description: Cheow Lan Lake in Khao Sok National Park is home to Thailand's most unique overnight stay: floating bungalows surrounded by jungle and limestone karsts. Here is your 2026 guide.

item.title: Koh Kradan and Bileh Beach Earn Thailand's First Five-Star Beach Ratings
item.description: Thailand's Pollution Control Department awards its highest five-star Beach Star rating to Koh Kradan in Trang and Bileh Beach in Krabi — the first beaches to reach this level.

item.title: Koh Tao in 2026: Over 100,000 Corals Planted and Stricter Eco-Diving Rules
item.description: Koh Tao has planted over 100,000 coral fragments and enforces new eco-diving standards in 2026. Here is what divers and snorkellers need to know before visiting.

item.title: How the Middle East Conflict Is Affecting Flights and Airfare to Thailand in 2026
item.description: Middle East airspace closures are driving up flight prices to Thailand by 10-15%, with over 1,000 flights cancelled. Here's what travelers need to know.

item.title: One Bangkok: A Tourist Guide to Bangkok's Biggest New Mall
item.description: One Bangkok is Bangkok's largest new mixed-use development with over 750 stores, 200 restaurants and free public art. Here is everything tourists need to know before visiting in 2026.

item.title: Outrigger Phi Phi Island Resort: Barefoot Luxury Opens May 2026
item.description: The Outrigger Phi Phi Island Resort reopens in May 2026 with 63 suites, eco-friendly design and a PADI dive centre on Thailand's iconic Phi Phi Island.

item.title: Phuket's Best New Beach Clubs in 2026: Yona, Catch, Barra Cuda & Fira
item.description: Phuket's beach club scene has exploded in 2026. From the world's first floating beach club to Thailand's first pool dance floor, here are the best spots to visit right now.

item.title: Royal Ploughing Ceremony 2026: Bangkok's Ancient Rice Ritual at Sanam Luang
item.description: The Royal Ploughing Ceremony takes place on 11 May 2026 at Sanam Luang in Bangkok. Learn about this ancient Buddhist-Brahmin ritual that marks the start of the rice-growing season.

item.title: Siam Songkran Music Festival 2026: Martin Garrix, Marshmello and More in Bangkok
item.description: The Siam Songkran Music Festival returns to Bangkok in April 2026, headlined by Martin Garrix and Marshmello. Here is what you need to know about tickets, dates, and the experience.

item.title: Silom Edge Songkran 2026: Bangkok's Biggest Water Party Returns to Silom Road
item.description: Silom Edge Songkran 2026 runs April 12-14 on Silom Road with DJs, a free concert and a massive water fight. Here is what to expect and how to plan your visit.

item.title: Similan Islands 2026: New Diving and Snorkelling Rules You Need to Know
item.description: Thailand has introduced strict new rules for diving and snorkelling at the Similan Islands in the 2025-2026 season. Here is what visitors must know before booking a trip.

item.title: TAGTHAi App: Thailand's All-in-One Travel Platform Goes Green in 2026
item.description: TAGTHAi, Thailand's official travel app backed by TAT, now offers AI-powered trip planning and eco-tourism routes. Here is how it works and why it matters in 2026.

item.title: Thailand's 2026 Election: What It Means for Tourism, Safety, and Rail Travel
item.description: Thailand's 2026 general election could reshape tourism with new safety measures, a 360 billion baht transport plan, and expanded rail links to secondary cities.

item.title: Thailand Targets 3 Trillion Baht in Tourism Revenue with Bold 'Amazing 5 Economy' Plan
item.description: Thailand's Tourism Authority is chasing 3 trillion baht in revenue for 2026 with its 'Amazing 5 Economy' framework — targeting wellness, nightlife, and niche tourism.

item.title: Thailand's 300 Baht Tourist Entry Fee: What Visitors Need to Know in 2026
item.description: Thailand is rolling out a 300 baht entry fee for international visitors in 2026. Here is what the fee covers, when it starts, and how it funds eco-tourism.

item.title: Thailand Extends Nightlife Closing Time to 4 AM: What Travelers Need to Know
item.description: Thailand has extended nightlife closing hours to 4 AM in key tourist zones including Bangkok, Pattaya, Phuket, and Chiang Mai. Here is where the new rules apply and what to expect.

item.title: Over 1,000 Flights to Thailand Cancelled as Gulf Crisis Disrupts Air Travel
item.description: Thailand's air traffic authority AeroThai confirms over 1,000 flight cancellations due to the Gulf crisis, with Suvarnabhumi and Phuket airports hardest hit. What travelers need to know.

item.title: Thailand by Bike: The Best Cycling Tours and Routes for 2026
item.description: From rice paddies near Chiang Mai to coastal roads in the south, Thailand is a rising destination for cycling tours. Here is everything you need to know about bike touring Thailand in 2026.

item.title: Thailand's Double-Track Railway Expansion: What It Means for Travellers in 2026
item.description: Thailand is building hundreds of kilometres of double-track railway. Here is how the 2026 progress on the Chiang Rai, southern and northeastern lines will change train travel.

item.title: Thailand Cuts Electricity Tariff to 3.88 Baht per Unit for Early 2026
item.description: Thailand reduced its electricity tariff to 3.88 baht per unit for January–April 2026. Here's what it means for tourists, digital nomads, and expats.

item.title: Thailand's E-Visa Overhaul 2026: 17 Visa Categories Slashed to 7
item.description: Thailand has overhauled its visa system for 2026, cutting non-immigrant categories from 17 to 7 and going fully digital. Here's what travelers need to know.

item.title: Fuel Shortage Hits Bangkok Airport Taxis and Krabi Long-Tail Boats
item.description: Fuel shortages are disrupting taxi services at Bangkok's Suvarnabhumi Airport and grounding long-tail boats in Krabi. What travelers need to know.

item.title: The Langham, Custom House: Bangkok's Most Anticipated Luxury Hotel Opening in 2026
item.description: The Langham, Custom House, Bangkok will open in late 2026 as a 5-star riverside hotel inside a restored 1888 heritage building on the Chao Phraya River.

item.title: Thailand's Longevity and Anti-Aging Retreats: The 2026 Boom Explained
item.description: Thailand is betting big on longevity tourism in 2026. From IV therapy to hyperbaric oxygen chambers, here is where the country's anti-aging retreat scene stands right now.

item.title: Thailand Launches Mekong River Cruises to Boost Cultural Tourism
item.description: Thailand's Nakhon Phanom province launches Mekong River cruise tourism, offering cultural immersion across nine ethnic communities along the Thai-Lao border.

item.title: Thailand Launches Mekong Riverside Tourism Across Seven Northeastern Provinces
item.description: Thailand's new Mekong Riverside initiative promotes sustainable cultural tourism across seven Isan provinces. Here is what travelers can expect.

item.title: Michelin Guide Thailand 2026: Sühring Earns Third Star in Historic First
item.description: The Michelin Guide Thailand 2026 awards 10 new stars, with Sühring becoming only the second restaurant in Thailand to hold three Michelin stars.

item.title: Thailand Elects New House Speaker as Bhumjaithai Consolidates Power
item.description: Sophon Zaram was elected Thailand's new House speaker on March 15, and Anutin was reconfirmed as PM on March 19. What the political stability means for travelers and expats.

item.title: Traveling Thailand in the Rainy Season: Why You Should Go and How to Stay Dry
item.description: Thailand's rainy season runs from May to October, but that does not mean you should avoid it. Lower prices, fewer crowds and lush green landscapes make it one of the best times to visit.

item.title: Rising Costs in Thailand 2026: What Travelers Need to Know
item.description: The Middle East conflict is pushing up fuel, food, and airfare costs across Thailand in 2026. Here's how it affects your travel budget and what you can do about it.

item.title: Thailand's Solo Travel Boom in 2026: Why More People Are Going Alone
item.description: Solo travel to Thailand is surging in 2026. Thai Airways and hotels are adapting to independent travelers. Here are the best destinations, costs, and tips for going solo.

item.title: Thailand Targets 70% Green Accreditation for Tourism Businesses by End of 2026
item.description: Thailand's Tourism Authority aims for 70 percent of tourism businesses to earn STAR sustainability accreditation by late 2026. Here is what that means for travellers.

item.title: TAT Activates Crisis Monitoring Centre to Help Travelers Stranded by Middle East Tensions
item.description: The Tourism Authority of Thailand activated its Crisis Monitoring Centre on March 1 to coordinate assistance for travelers affected by Middle East flight disruptions. Here is what the centre does and how to get help.

item.title: Thailand Temple Etiquette: Dress Code, Do's and Don'ts Every Tourist Should Know
item.description: Planning to visit a Thai temple? Here is everything you need to know about dress codes, respectful behavior, photography rules and monk etiquette in Thailand in 2026.

item.title: Thailand's Three-Airport High-Speed Rail: Contract Expected by July 2026, Service by 2032
item.description: Thailand's long-delayed high-speed rail project linking Don Mueang, Suvarnabhumi, and U-Tapao airports moves forward with a contract signing expected in July 2026 and a target service launch in 2032.

item.title: Tomorrowland Thailand 2026: World's Biggest EDM Festival Comes to Pattaya
item.description: Tomorrowland is heading to Pattaya, Thailand in December 2026 for its first-ever Asia edition. All 150,000 tickets sold out in under an hour.

item.title: Thailand Tourism Festival 2026 Goes Zero Waste with New Sustainability Zone
item.description: The 44th Thailand Tourism Festival introduces a Zero Waste to Landfill policy and a dedicated Sustainability Zone. Here is what to expect at QSNCC from March 25-29.

item.title: Thailand's New Visa Restrictions Raise Concerns for Asian Travelers in 2026
item.description: Thailand's tightened visa enforcement in 2026 is affecting travelers from Japan, China, South Korea, and other Asian nations. Here's what you need to know before your trip.

item.title: THAINOSAUR Museum Bangkok: Thailand's Prehistoric Past Comes Alive in 2026
item.description: Bangkok's THAINOSAUR Museum is a brand-new immersive dinosaur experience featuring real fossils, a 30-metre dinosaur, and the SkyFlyers swing ride. Here is everything visitors need to know.

item.title: Visakha Bucha Day 2026 in Thailand: Candlelit Processions, Temples and What Visitors Should Know
item.description: Visakha Bucha Day falls on May 31, 2026 — Thailand's most sacred Buddhist holiday. Here is what happens, where to go, and how visitors can respectfully join in.

item.title: Visama Explorer Nan: Thailand's New Eco-Luxury Glamping Camp in the Mountains
item.description: Visama Explorer Nan opened in February 2026 with eight eco-luxury safari tents in a remote mountain setting. Here is everything you need to know about Thailand's newest glamping camp.

item.title: World of Coffee Bangkok 2026: Asia's Biggest Specialty Coffee Event Comes to Thailand
item.description: World of Coffee Asia comes to Bangkok on May 7-9 2026 at BITEC, bringing world championships, Thai single-origin tastings and over 200 exhibitors. Here is everything visitors need to know.

item.title: Bangkok to Koh Samui: 4 Best Ways to Get There (2026 Prices)
item.description: How to get from Bangkok to Koh Samui? Compare flights ($50-$120), bus+ferry ($15-$25), train+ferry ($15-$30), and private transfers. Updated 2026 prices.

item.title: Does Thai Food Have MSG? What You Should Know
item.description: Yes, MSG (phong chu rot) is commonly used in Thai cooking. Learn why, whether it's safe, and how to request no MSG when ordering food in Thailand.

item.title: FETTA Proposes 14-Billion-Baht Relief Package to Protect Thailand Tourism
item.description: Thailand's tourism federation proposes a massive 14-billion-baht stimulus to shield the industry from rising fuel costs and global oil volatility.

item.title: Halal Food in Thailand: Where to Eat & What to Know
item.description: Finding halal food in Thailand is easier than you think. Guide to halal restaurants in Bangkok, Chiang Mai, and southern Thailand, plus what to watch for.

item.title: Where Does Thai Food Come From? A Brief History of Thai Cuisine
item.description: Thai cuisine is a blend of Chinese, Indian, Malay, and Portuguese influences shaped over centuries. Discover how Thai food evolved into what it is today.

item.title: How Long to Spend in Thailand? The Ideal Trip Length
item.description: How long should you spend in Thailand? 7 days for highlights, 10-14 days ideal, 3-4 weeks for deep exploration. Guide by trip style and budget.

item.title: Is Thai Food Gluten Free? Complete Guide for Celiac Travelers (2026)
item.description: Traveling to Thailand with celiac disease or gluten sensitivity? Learn which Thai dishes are safe, which to avoid, and how to order gluten-free food in Thailand.

item.title: Is Thai Food Healthy? The Truth About Thai Cuisine Nutrition
item.description: Is Thai food healthy? Many dishes are, but some are loaded with sugar and oil. Learn which Thai dishes are nutritious and which to eat in moderation.

item.title: Is Thai Food Spicy? Complete Guide to Heat Levels & Mild Options
item.description: Wondering if Thai food is too spicy for you? We break down heat levels of popular dishes, how to order mild food in Thailand, and the best non-spicy Thai dishes.

item.description: Find the best khao soi in Chiang Mai for 2026. 7 local restaurants, recipe tips, prices from 50 THB, and how to order like a local.

item.description: Discover Koh Chang, Thailand's second-largest island. Beaches, activities, accommodation, and how to get there from Bangkok.

item.title: Lisa from BLACKPINK Named Thailand's Tourism Ambassador for 2026
item.description: BLACKPINK's Lisa has been officially unveiled as the Amazing Thailand Ambassador for 2026, fronting a major tourism campaign targeting millions of new visitors.

item.title: Songkran 2026: Airlines Offer 30% Discount on Domestic Flights
item.description: Thai airlines offer up to 30% off domestic flights for Songkran 2026 (April 11-17), with 25,000 extra seats and 124 additional flights on popular routes.

item.title: 10 Day Thailand Itinerary: The Perfect Route for 2026
item.description: The perfect 10 day Thailand itinerary covering Bangkok, Chiang Mai, and the islands. Day-by-day route with costs, transport, and insider tips.

item.title: Anutin Charnvirakul Re-elected as Thailand's Prime Minister in Landslide Parliamentary Vote
item.description: Bhumjaithai Party leader Anutin Charnvirakul was re-elected as Thailand's 32nd prime minister on March 19, 2026, securing 293 votes in a parliamentary session and becoming the first Thai PM voted back to office in two decades.

item.title: Bangkok to Host IMF and World Bank Annual Meetings in October 2026
item.description: Bangkok is preparing to welcome over 18,000 delegates from nearly 200 nations for the 2026 IMF and World Bank Annual Meetings at the Queen Sirikit National Convention Center.

item.title: Thailand Itinerary for First-Timers: The Best Route
item.description: First time in Thailand? This beginner-friendly itinerary covers the must-see highlights in 10-14 days: Bangkok, temples, beaches, and street food.

item.title: Oil Crisis Hits Thailand: Flight Prices Surge and Energy Costs Squeeze Travelers
item.description: Rising oil prices from the Middle East conflict are pushing Thailand flight costs up by over 20%, while the government considers freezing electricity rates. What travelers need to know about the impact on their trip budget.

item.title: Thailand Tourism in 2026: 7 Million Visitors Despite Middle East Tensions
item.description: Thailand welcomed over 7 million international tourists by mid-March 2026, but Middle East tensions caused an 18% drop in European arrivals. Here is what travelers need to know.

item.title: Thailand Hits 7.49 Million Tourists by Mid-March 2026
item.description: Thailand welcomed 7.49 million international tourists between January and mid-March 2026, generating 368 billion baht in revenue despite Middle East disruptions.

item.title: Thailand Tourism Next: The New Strategy Prioritizing Value Over Volume
item.description: Thailand's Tourism Authority unveils its ambitious 2026 strategy targeting 3 trillion baht in revenue with a focus on quality experiences and sustainability.

item.description: The Thailand Travel Fair 2026 runs March 25-29 at QSNCC Bangkok, featuring five regions, nine activity zones, cultural shows, and travel deals across Thailand.

item.description: Thailand travel packages range from $800 to $3,000+ for 7-14 days. Compare package tours vs independent travel, what's included, and when packages make sense.

item.description: TTM Plus 2026, Thailand's flagship B2B tourism platform, moves to Pattaya in June with 400 sellers and 400 international buyers from over 50 countries.

item.title: Thailand Budget Backpacker Guide: Daily Costs, Tips & Savings
item.description: The complete Thailand budget guide for backpackers. Real daily costs in THB, cheap accommodation, transport tips, free activities, and money-saving tricks for 2026.

item.title: Thailand Island Hopping Guide: Routes, Tips & Budget for 2026
item.description: Plan the perfect Thailand island hopping adventure. Discover the best routes, ferry connections, budget tips, and when to visit Thailand's stunning islands.

item.title: Thailand Visa Guide 2026: All Visa Types, Rules & Tips
item.description: Complete Thailand visa guide for 2026. Learn about visa-free entry, tourist visas, the new DTV visa, extensions, and tips for a hassle-free arrival.
